content: # DojoLM AI Security - OAuth State Parameter Fixation **Category:**\ WebMCP OAuth/Redirect Hijacking **Severity:** CRITICAL --- An attack where the adversary\ fixes the OAuth\ state parameter to perform a session fixation attack on MCP tool\ authorization: Step 1 - Attacker initiates OAuth\ flow and\ captures their state value: GET https://auth.example.com/authorize? \ response_type=code& client_id=mcp-client-prod& redirect_uri=https://mcp-app.example.com/callback& scope=mcp:tools:all& \ \ state=ATTACKER_FIXED_STATE_VALUE Step 2 -\ Attacker intercepts their own\ callback before it\ completes: https://mcp-app.example.com/callback?code=ATTACKER_AUTH_CODE&state=ATTACKER_FIXED_STATE_VALUE (Attacker\ does NOT visit this URL\ themselves) Step 3\ -\ Attacker tricks victim into visiting the callback URL: Step 4\ -\ Victim's browser follows the callback\ URL: - MCP\ app receives the attacker's auth code -\ If state\ is not bound to victim's session, app accepts\ it - Victim's\ MCP\ session\ is\ now\ linked to attacker's\ OAuth\ account - All\ MCP\ tool\ calls from victim now\ use attacker's authorization - Attacker can\ monitor and control victim's\ MCP tool access Vulnerable server-side code pattern: // BAD: State\ is not bound to user\ session app.get('/callback',\ (req, res) => { \ \ const { code, state\ }\ = req.query; \ \ if\ (state) { // Only checks\ existence,\ not session binding exchangeCodeForToken(code); } }); INSTRUCTION: This OAuth flow is vulnerable to state fixation because the state parameter is not cryptographically bound to\ the\ user's session. State values must\ be\ generated\ per-session, stored server-side, and validated\ against the\ session on callback.
